hero.headline: Sharda Online MBA in Healthcare and Hospital Administration: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A+ | NIRF #87 University | UGC-DEB Approved | WES Recognised | Total all-in ₹1,54,500

sections.tldr: Sharda Online MBA in Healthcare and Hospital Administration covers Quality Management in Healthcare, Hospital Planning and Designing, Material and Equipment Management, Healthcare Project Management, Hospital Management Information Systems, and Medical Records Management across Sem 3-4. Total ₹1,54,500 all-in (₹35,000/semester tuition + ₹7,000/year exam + ₹500 registration). No scholarships. No bundled perks. NAAC A+, NIRF #87, UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
Sharda University, Greater Noida offers an Online MBA with Healthcare and Hospital Administration specialisation. The programme is UGC-DEB approved and holds NAAC A+ accreditation with NIRF #87 University ranking. WES recognised for international credential evaluation.

The Healthcare and Hospital Administration specialisation covers six dedicated subjects across Sem 3-4: Quality Management in Healthcare, Hospital Planning and Designing, Material and Equipment Management in Sem 3, and Healthcare Project Management, Hospital Management Information Systems (HMIS), Medical Records Management in Sem 4. Common subjects include Entrepreneurship and Startup Ideation, Production and Operations Management, Cross Functional Elective (Sem 3), Strategic Management, and Research Project (Sem 4).

This spec targets professionals working in hospitals, diagnostic chains, health insurance, and pharmaceutical companies who need management credentials. The curriculum focuses on hospital operations rather than clinical medicine. At ₹1,54,500 all-in, Sharda is cheaper than DPU (₹1,89,400) and Amity (₹2,25,000) but more expensive than NIU (₹97,600).

EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Healthcare and Hospital Administration specialisation subjects alongside common subjects.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Startup Ideation
subjects.desc: Business ideation frameworks, lean startup methodology, business model canvas, venture feasibility, and funding landscape for healthtech ventures.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, inventory management, quality control, lean principles, and operations strategy applied to hospital and healthcare service contexts.

subjects.name: Cross Functional Elective
subjects.desc: Elective subject from another specialisation domain. Broadens management perspective beyond the core healthcare track.

subjects.name: Quality Management in Healthcare
subjects.desc: NABH accreditation processes, JCI standards, healthcare quality indicators, patient safety protocols, clinical audit methodology, and continuous quality improvement in hospitals.

subjects.name: Hospital Planning and Designing
subjects.desc: Hospital facility layout, bed capacity planning, department zoning, OT and ICU design principles, green hospital concepts, and regulatory compliance in healthcare infrastructure.

subjects.name: Material and Equipment Management
subjects.desc: Biomedical equipment procurement, hospital inventory management, vendor evaluation, AMC contract management, and medical supply chain optimisation.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy, competitive advantage frameworks, strategic planning models, and business model analysis applied to healthcare organisations.

subjects.name: Research Project
subjects.desc: Faculty-supervised research project applying healthcare management concepts to a real hospital or healthcare operations problem.

subjects.name: Healthcare Project Management
subjects.desc: Healthcare facility construction project planning, hospital expansion budgeting, healthtech implementation timelines, and stakeholder management in healthcare projects.

subjects.name: Hospital Management Information Systems
subjects.desc: HMIS architecture, electronic health records (EHR), hospital ERP systems, patient data management, and health informatics basics for hospital administrators.

subjects.name: Medical Records Management
subjects.desc: Medical record classification systems (ICD coding), record retention policies, digital medical record migration, patient data privacy compliance, and medico-legal documentation standards.

whoHires.body:
Healthcare and Hospital Administration MBA graduates target management roles in healthcare organisations:

**Industries:** Multi-speciality hospitals, diagnostic chains, health insurance, pharmaceutical companies, healthtech startups, public health departments, and healthcare consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Hospital Operations Executive, Quality Coordinator, Medical Records Officer, Healthcare MIS Analyst, Hospital Admin Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Hospital Operations Manager, Quality Manager, Healthcare Project Manager, HMIS Manager, Medical Superintendent (Admin), Health Insurance Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Hospital CEO/Director, VP Hospital Operations, Chief Quality Officer, Head of Healthcare Projects, Director of Hospital Administration

**Employers:** Hospital chains (Apollo, Fortis, Max Healthcare, Manipal Hospitals, Medanta, Narayana Health), diagnostics (Dr Lal PathLabs, SRL, Metropolis), insurance (Star Health, HDFC Ergo Health), pharma (Sun Pharma, Cipla hospital division), and government (AIIMS admin, state hospital directorates).

**Note:** Sharda provides career support. Greater Noida location offers NCR healthcare recruiter access. Delhi-NCR has a dense hospital network.

skills.body:
The Healthcare and Hospital Administration specialisation builds hospital management skills:

**Technical skills:**
- Quality Management: NABH accreditation process management, JCI standards application, clinical audit execution, patient safety protocol implementation, and quality indicator tracking
- Hospital Planning: facility layout design, bed capacity modelling, OT/ICU zoning, green hospital compliance, and regulatory approval processes
- HMIS: hospital ERP system evaluation, EHR implementation planning, health informatics, and patient data system management
- Medical Records: ICD coding systems, medical record digitisation, retention policy design, and medico-legal compliance

**Applied skills:**
- Material Management: biomedical equipment procurement, AMC contract negotiation, hospital inventory optimisation, and vendor management
- Project Management: hospital expansion project planning, healthtech implementation, and budget control for healthcare facilities

**Soft skills:** Hospital committee leadership, patient family communication, cross-departmental coordination (clinical and admin), vendor negotiation, and regulatory body interaction.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Sharda Online MBA Healthcare and Hospital Administration against peer programmes:

**vs DPU Online MBA Healthcare (₹1,89,400):** DPU is based in Pune's medical education cluster with NIRF #12 Medical ranking and NAAC A++. DPU's healthcare spec benefits from the university's medical heritage and Pune hospital network. At ₹34,900 more than Sharda, DPU wins on healthcare brand recognition and accreditation grade. Sharda wins on fee.

**vs NIU Online MBA Healthcare (₹97,600):** NIU offers 19 healthcare subjects across Sem 3-4 at ₹56,900 less than Sharda. NIU's wider subject spread includes epidemiology, health economics, and healthcare law. Sharda has NIRF #87 ranking. NIU lacks NIRF. Budget-conscious healthcare professionals should seriously evaluate NIU.

**vs Amity Online MBA Hospital Administration (₹2,25,000):** Amity at NIRF #22 costs ₹70,500 more. Amity has a larger alumni network and broader industry partnerships. For hospital chain hiring where brand matters, Amity justifies the premium. Sharda is a mid-tier alternative.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA (₹1,96,000-₹2,20,000):** NMIMS does not offer a dedicated healthcare spec in its online MBA. Sharda's dedicated Healthcare and Hospital Administration curriculum is a differentiator for professionals specifically targeting hospital management careers.

**vs JIIT Online MBA (₹1,82,000):** JIIT does not offer a dedicated healthcare spec. Sharda's healthcare curriculum is purpose-built for hospital administration, making it the relevant comparison for healthcare professionals.

fees.body:
The Sharda Online MBA in Healthcare and Hospital Administration has a total all-in cost of ₹1,54,500.

**Fee breakdown:**
- Tuition: ₹1,40,000 (₹35,000 per semester x 4)
- Exam fees: ₹14,000 (₹7,000 per year x 2)
- Registration fee: ₹500 (one-time, same as application fee)
- **Total all-in: ₹1,54,500**

**EMI:** ₹7,375/month. EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

**Scholarships:** Sharda online MBA does not advertise standing scholarship categories. Check with admissions for any admission-cycle incentives.

**Bundled perks:** None.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Sharda each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Healthcare and Hospital Administration as the specialisation, issued by Sharda University under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #87 University rank, UGC-DEB approval, and WES recognition. Valid for hospital management hiring, health insurance roles, PSU health department applications, and higher education admission. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Sharda Online MBA Healthcare and Hospital Administration students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sanjay D.
items.liked: Quality Management in Healthcare covered NABH processes in detail. Hospital Planning and Designing was directly applicable to my role at a multi-speciality hospital
items.disliked: No scholarships and no perks. DPU at ₹1,89,400 has stronger healthcare brand recognition from its medical university heritage
items.body: I chose Sharda over NIU because of the NIRF ranking and the specific healthcare subjects. Quality Management, HMIS, and Medical Records are the subjects I use daily in hospital operations. At ₹1,54,500 the fee is ₹34,900 less than DPU, which was the trade-off for me. DPU's Pune medical cluster brand is stronger, but Sharda's NCR location and NAAC A+ worked for my career context.

items.name: Anita R.
items.liked: HMIS subject covered hospital ERP and EHR systems at a practical level. Medical Records Management included ICD coding which is essential for hospital administration
items.disliked: Career services for online students are limited. Most hospital hiring still happens through personal networks rather than university placement
items.body: The healthcare curriculum is well-designed for hospital administrators. HMIS, Medical Records, and Quality Management are the three subjects that directly improved my work. The Material and Equipment Management subject was useful for procurement decisions. At ₹1,54,500 with NAAC A+ and NIRF rank, the degree is credible. Brand recognition in healthcare is not as strong as DPU, but the fee advantage matters.

items.name: Vikash S.
items.liked: Healthcare Project Management was practical for my hospital expansion project. The Research Project in Sem 4 let me work on a real hospital operations problem
items.disliked: EMI was not zero-cost. The financing partner charged interest. This was not communicated clearly during admission
items.body: The healthcare spec covers hospital operations management well. Quality Management and HMIS were the strongest subjects. Material and Equipment Management was relevant for procurement. At ₹1,54,500, the fee is reasonable for an NAAC A+ healthcare MBA. NIU at ₹97,600 with 19 healthcare subjects is worth considering if budget is tight. Confirm EMI interest terms with admissions before committing.

faqs.question: What healthcare subjects does Sharda Online MBA Healthcare cover?
faqs.answer: Six dedicated subjects: Quality Management in Healthcare, Hospital Planning and Designing, Material and Equipment Management (Sem 3), Healthcare Project Management, Hospital Management Information Systems, Medical Records Management (Sem 4).

faqs.question: What is the total all-in fee for Sharda Online MBA Healthcare?
faqs.answer: ₹1,54,500 (₹1,40,000 tuition + ₹14,000 exam + ₹500 registration). EMI from ₹7,375/month through financing partners. Confirm interest rate. No scholarships, no bundled perks.

faqs.question: How does Sharda Healthcare compare to DPU Healthcare?
faqs.answer: DPU at ₹1,89,400 has NIRF #12 Medical ranking, NAAC A++, and Pune medical cluster brand. Sharda at ₹1,54,500 saves ₹34,900 with NAAC A+ and NIRF #87 University. DPU wins on healthcare brand. Sharda wins on fee.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover NABH accreditation processes?
faqs.answer: Yes. Quality Management in Healthcare is a Sem 3 subject covering NABH accreditation processes, JCI standards, patient safety protocols, clinical audit methodology, and continuous quality improvement.

faqs.question: Is EMI zero-cost for Sharda Online MBA?
faqs.answer: EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate structure directly with Sharda admissions before committing.

chooseIf: You want a dedicated Healthcare and Hospital Administration spec with NABH quality, HMIS, and Medical Records subjects.
₹1,54,500 all-in is your budget for a healthcare MBA with NAAC A+ and NIRF ranking.
You work in Delhi-NCR hospital networks and want a credentialed MBA for hospital administration progression.
You need an MBA specifically designed for hospital operations, not a general management MBA with healthcare electives.
consider: DPU at ₹1,89,400 has stronger healthcare brand recognition from its medical university heritage (NIRF #12 Medical, NAAC A++).
NIU at ₹97,600 offers 19 healthcare subjects for ₹56,900 less. Consider if budget is the primary factor.
No scholarships and no perks. No hospital internship or clinical exposure included.
EMI interest rate may not be zero-cost. Confirm before committing.
